antecedent_audit.policy: Repository citations remain pinned to evidence_head_sha; current heads are drift context only.

implementations: The target has one top-level controller and preserves the ten internal agents as independent child tools.
major_gaps: Final package round-trip proof remains blocked on packaging artifacts.
capabilities.purpose: Keep orchestration separate from child capabilities.
required_tests: Inventory agents before/after packaging and prove independent execution/isolation.
security_notes: Controller authority must not leak into child tools.

implementations: AST extraction, SHA-256, provenance fields, version immutability, CI, and approval exist.
major_gaps: SDK install does not enforce the recorded hash; scanners have broad exemptions.
capabilities.purpose: Build and publish a canonical single-file agent index.
required_tests: Verify full hash at install, publisher policy, immutable version, and malicious-agent rejection.
security_notes: Registry approval is not a sandbox or publisher signature.

implementations: A catalog, validator, workflow, and six senses exist.
major_gaps: Validator permits executable top-level behavior and root license is absent.
capabilities.purpose: Add output rendering without changing the agent core.
required_tests: AST allowlist, no top-level effects, exact hash, provenance, and rendering isolation.
security_notes: Importing a sense is arbitrary Python execution.

implementations: Many real single-file agents and stack manifests exist.
major_gaps: Dependencies and trust metadata are not uniformly locked.
protocol_or_contract: RAPP BasicAgent and RAR cartridge conventions
capabilities.purpose: Make one capability portable as one reviewed Python file.
required_tests: No undeclared imports; deterministic metadata; ABI and standalone invocation tests.
security_notes: Single-file form reduces packaging, not execution risk.

implementations: Prompt/plugin skill packs exist but do not implement the BasicAgent wire.
major_gaps: Ecosystem prose often calls skills agents or capabilities interchangeably.
capabilities.purpose: Keep host instructions distinct from runtime agents.
required_tests: Reject skill-only artifacts from the agent manifest and execution loader.
security_notes: A skill must not gain runtime authority through naming alone.

implementations: Named stacks, manifests, loader, and activation patterns exist.
major_gaps: Remote/mutable stack loading and dependency policy vary.
capabilities.purpose: Activate a named collection of reviewed agents.
required_tests: Stack closure, collisions, missing agent, exact hash, and rollback tests.
security_notes: Activation may only reference approved local manifest entries.

implementations: The envelope is specified and emitted by bridge scripts.
major_gaps: Legacy bridges use unverified from strings and no replay cache.
capabilities.purpose: Address a typed request from one RAPPID to another.
required_tests: Complete/missing fields, wrong destination, timestamps, nonce, kind, payload, and facets.
security_notes: Every required field must be signed and destination-bound.

implementations: Runnable handlers exist.
major_gaps: Wire drift and excessive route surfaces remain upstream.
capabilities.purpose: Carry local owner conversation into the runtime.
required_tests: Loopback IPv4/IPv6, remote rejection, exact route, request bounds, and response schema.
security_notes: Request bounds, origin/Host policy, and local ownership are mandatory.

implementations: Hatch/list/boot/stop/chat/parallel/job operations are implemented.
major_gaps: Draft protocol and unsafe port ownership remain.
capabilities.purpose: Fan one request to project twins and aggregate jobs.
required_tests: Partial failure, timeout, duplicate job, wrong child, restart, and aggregation tests.
security_notes: Fan-out requires signed child identity and idempotent job state.

implementations: Nonce fields and durable delivery concepts exist, but no upstream proves the selected algorithm.
major_gaps: Target durable exactly-once implementation is absent.
capabilities.purpose: Prevent duplicate/conflicting signed requests from repeating effects.
required_tests: Duplicate same digest, nonce conflict, stale request, crash phases, corruption, fsync failure, and concurrency.
security_notes: Journal failure must fail closed before any side effect.

implementations: Browser, Python, and Azure signing/verification code exists.
major_gaps: Key encodings, kinds, replay, size, and identity semantics differ.
capabilities.purpose: Authenticate the complete inner request at relay boundaries.
required_tests: Canonical wrapper, signature, from/key/RAPPID binding, kind/size, and tamper vectors.
security_notes: A relay transports events but does not become sender authority.

implementations: Response envelopes are described/emitted, but signing is not uniformly required.
major_gaps: No selected implementation proves response signature and nonce binding.
capabilities.purpose: Bind the reply to the authenticated request.
required_tests: Swapped participants, request nonce, signature, digest, replay, and wrong-responder tests.
security_notes: Unsigned replies permit substitution even when requests are authenticated.

implementations: Health/conversation/Copilot Studio endpoints and storage are implemented.
major_gaps: Dynamic Python from storage and reflected credentialed CORS appear in implementations.
capabilities.purpose: Run agent/memory endpoints as an always-on cloud body.
required_tests: Auth, CORS, fixed code, tenant isolation, storage least privilege, and deployment rollback.
security_notes: Cloud execution is outside v1 and requires fixed code and managed identity.

implementations: A substantial runtime, deployment assets, installers, and tests exist.
major_gaps: Some handoffs clone a different repository and no full local-to-M365 attestation exists.
capabilities.purpose: Progress local memory and agents into Azure.
required_tests: Local/cloud parity, immutable handoff, privacy classification, and rollback.
security_notes: Export must never include local private state.

implementations: Converters and checked-in/generator deployment artifacts exist.
major_gaps: Historical package names, missing repositories, and mutable defaults drift.
capabilities.purpose: Convert a rapplication into a Power Platform solution.
required_tests: Deterministic solution, schema import, no secrets, exact source, and environment cleanup.
security_notes: Generated solutions need provenance, checksums, and no embedded credentials.

implementations: Spec, CLI, static twin, deterministic encoding, and service-to-service hatcher exist.
major_gaps: Browser implementation only truly executes a small built-in subset.
capabilities.purpose: Encode agents and deterministic vTwins in Dataverse.
required_tests: Deterministic record IDs, HTTPS, auth audience, CRUD parity, rollback, and no token storage.
security_notes: CLI must require HTTPS and least-privilege token audience.

implementations: Deployable BOMs, TypeSpec, WorkIQ agents, and dashboard rapplication exist.
major_gaps: CI, identity, licensing, and end-to-end behavior vary.
capabilities.purpose: Present capabilities in Teams/Microsoft 365 agents.
required_tests: Manifest validation, permission review, tenant isolation, revocation, and no local-state export.
security_notes: M365 permissions and customer data need explicit least privilege.

implementations: Device-code/service-principal auth, converter, importer, and browser surface are real.
major_gaps: Default sources track mutable branches; browser setup is not literally one click.
capabilities.purpose: Authenticate, discover Dataverse, and import a solution.
required_tests: Wrong tenant, checksum mismatch, expired auth, partial import, rollback, and least privilege.
security_notes: Bind the imported solution to an immutable checksum and explicit tenant/environment.

implementations: Multiple brainstem/twin egg generations exist.
Local deterministic implementation and stdlib tests exist; final public release attestation remains separate.
major_gaps: No public implementation proves this exact target subtype and all required checks.
capabilities.purpose: Carry the selected rapplication as an offline install artifact.
required_tests: Schema, digest, signature policy, round-trip, limits, and wrong-family rejection.
security_notes: The target subtype must not accept generic same-suffix eggs.

implementations: The cart contract is normative; RACon is a partial browser prototype.
major_gaps: No host, vectors, or conformance suite proves the cart promise.
capabilities.purpose: Promise one-gesture insert, boot, use, and eject.
required_tests: Gesture count, no setup, first-class use, clean eject, and malicious cart tests.
security_notes: Prototype extract-and-execute is unsafe for untrusted cartridges.

implementations: Two incompatible cubby schemas exist in direct evidence.
major_gaps: No existing schema is adopted wholesale; target schema/build is not implemented.
capabilities.purpose: Create the deterministic reviewed build boundary.
required_tests: Schema vectors, canonical bytes, reproducibility, privacy scan, and incompatible-cubby rejection.
security_notes: Cubby must exclude every private or unreviewed byte.

implementations: Frozen canary/dev trees and an old mutable-main version selector remain public.
major_gaps: They lag the current behavioral pointer and do not provide immutable safe installation.
capabilities.purpose: Identify installer paths that must not re-enter the selected chain.
required_tests: Assert no URL, source file, artifact, or workflow references a deprecated installer path.
security_notes: Mutable-main selection and credential/dependency copying are prohibited.

implementations: Build-once provenance and kernel pin patterns exist.
Local deterministic implementation and stdlib tests exist; final public release attestation remains separate.
major_gaps: Final exact-commit publication and public byte attestation remain unresolved.
capabilities.purpose: Reproduce every artifact byte from the pinned release.
required_tests: Two clean builders produce identical digests and manifests with network disabled after staging.
security_notes: Generated timestamps, mutable downloads, and hidden caches break reproducibility.

implementations: Scaffolders and organism distros exist.
major_gaps: Verifier permits missing files; installers consume mutable branches; families differ.
capabilities.purpose: Bundle kernel pins, identity, organs, senses, and installer policy.
required_tests: Kernel pin completeness, missing-file failure, immutable install, and no credential copy.
security_notes: Distro is not the same as OpenRappter or the source runtime.

implementations: The selected local path requires pinned signed release metadata and an expected digest, keeps archive verification/extraction on one descriptor, inventories RECORD/Python/imsg bytes, and fails atomically.
major_gaps: Public supported-host attestation remains external; the local hardened hatch is implemented and attack-tested.
capabilities.purpose: Materialize an egg into an isolated installed twin.
required_tests: Traversal, links, special files, bombs, wrong digest/signature, atomic failure, and policy authorization.
security_notes: Never expose hatch as an unconstrained LLM tool.

implementations: Backup/lay/summon and indexed egg workflows exist.
Local deterministic implementation and stdlib tests exist; final public release attestation remains separate.
major_gaps: Current trust is often self-asserted by the egg manifest.
capabilities.purpose: Restore a known release without network dependency.
required_tests: Network-disabled restore, independent signature/digest, identity binding, and recovery chat/replay tests.
security_notes: Offline verification needs an independent trust root and private-state policy.

implementations: Store validation, converter output, and a concrete RACon cartridge exist.
major_gaps: Existing bundles do not prove target controller/child semantics.
capabilities.purpose: Package a controller and real internal agents as one application.
required_tests: Manifest closure, independent agent discovery, controller-only streaming, deterministic round-trip.
security_notes: An application bundle cannot include runtime-private state.

implementations: Local build -> pinned signed verify -> hatch -> static verify-install -> controller adoption/start is tested byte-for-byte. Public upload/re-download proof remains unresolved.
major_gaps: No public path proves immutable publication through safe isolated hatch.
protocol_or_contract: RAPP Store plus target artifact chain
capabilities.purpose: Connect immutable public discovery to local installation.
required_tests: End-to-end publish/download/verify/hatch plus mutation, rollback, and offline fixtures.
security_notes: Catalog, artifact, publisher, and installed manifest all need separate verification.

implementations: Strict validators, bounded rounds, weakest-item selection, and persistence rules are tested.
major_gaps: Its cubby and Moment shapes conflict with other families and license is noncommercial.
capabilities.purpose: Iteratively improve bounded artifacts against validators.
required_tests: Bounds, duplicate keys, deterministic identity, policy limits, and incompatible Moment projection.
security_notes: Never adapt code without license/provenance clearance.

implementations: A normative wire, implemented engine/player, tests, and cartridge player exist.
major_gaps: Moment object shape and licensing conflict with Double Jump and other consumers.
capabilities.purpose: Render and evolve signed serverless Moments.
required_tests: Normative vectors, signature, renderer sandbox, deterministic cartridge, and cross-consumer projection.
security_notes: Remote cartridge hashes do not establish publisher trust.

implementations: Benchmark, golden, arena, and challenge import code exist.
major_gaps: Model restoration and executable challenge trust boundaries are weak.
capabilities.purpose: Measure latency, behavior, and agent evolution.
required_tests: State restoration, isolated sessions, fixed model, malicious challenge, and deterministic scoring.
security_notes: Benchmarks must restore state in finally and cannot import unreviewed challenges.

implementations: Concurrent fan-out and structured results exist.
major_gaps: Exactly-once signed aggregation and restart behavior are unproved.
capabilities.purpose: Combine signed per-node outcomes with partial-failure semantics.
required_tests: Timeout, partial failure, duplicate, conflicting node, restart, and signature tests.
security_notes: Aggregation must not trust node prose as control input.

implementations: The newer protocol claim maps fleet coordination to signed twin chat.
major_gaps: Current controller also exposes broader deploy/shell/direct-agent behavior.
capabilities.purpose: Carry bounded signed fleet operations over the canonical chat wire.
required_tests: Per-node key/RAPPID binding, replay, operation allowlist, response signature, and fan-out.
security_notes: Allowlisted operations only; no shell, deploy, import, or console.

implementations: The endpoint and client are implemented and documented as unauthenticated RCE.
major_gaps: No authentication or safe exposure boundary.
capabilities.purpose: Invoke a named agent directly on fleet nodes.
required_tests: Assert route absence and reject equivalent dynamic dispatch on every listener.
security_notes: Never expose or adapt this route.

implementations: Stdlib controller, direct-node endpoint, deployment, shell, routing, and MCP exist.
major_gaps: No tests/CI and dangerous operations share the implementation.
capabilities.purpose: Coordinate many no-LLM bodies from one controller.
required_tests: Enrollment, signed fan-out, no dangerous routes, concurrency, errors, and audit receipts.
security_notes: Treat the implementation as reference-only until separated and authenticated.

implementations: A live two-egg registry and self-contained hatcher/freeze agent exist.
major_gaps: No signature/hash verification and manifest-controlled filesystem paths.
capabilities.purpose: Freeze/hatch one multicellular operator being.
required_tests: Publisher signature, full digest, path containment, limits, identity, and family rejection.
security_notes: This artifact is not fleet authorization and is excluded from CUBBY.

implementations: Egg and social/spec fragments exist; some advertised factory/wrapper repositories are unpublished.
major_gaps: No complete direct public implementation chain exists in the census.
capabilities.purpose: Model one being as cells across estates.
required_tests: Resolve every source, schema, cell identity, estate relation, signature, and round-trip.
security_notes: Do not infer authority from the Leviathan name collision.

implementations: The target contract explicitly orders project lock, protocols, source, pointer, and references.
major_gaps: Final release identity is unresolved.
capabilities.purpose: Resolve contradictory claims deterministically.
required_tests: Inject contradictory fixtures and prove the selected rule wins.
security_notes: A canonical label never overrides direct pinned evidence.

implementations: A substantial doctrine corpus exists across species root and spine.
major_gaps: Current implementations violate several frozen wire and security claims.
capabilities.purpose: Describe invariants and ecosystem law.
required_tests: Trace every adopted MUST to an executable conformance test.
security_notes: Governance text is not executable enforcement.

implementations: Authenticated direct API metadata/exact heads plus eight checked-in deterministic shards cover all 307 public repositories at the exact cutoff; indexes remain subjects only.
major_gaps: The crawl does not execute repository code or inspect private repositories; changed antecedent heads retain old pinned evidence until directly re-audited.
capabilities.purpose: Ground synthesis in every public repository at its own head.
required_tests: Verify 307 unique names against raw inventory, exact heads/empties, cutoff, shard assignment/digests, and eight new-repository inspections.
security_notes: Direct evidence can still contain unreviewed copyrighted or unsafe material.

implementations: Installer head contains brainstem 0.6.16 and broad preflight definitions.
major_gaps: No root license and no usable 0.6.16 release tag at audit time.
capabilities.purpose: Identify the strongest current behavioral brainstem.
required_tests: Compare adapted behavior against permitted pointer vectors without copying bytes.
security_notes: Behavioral authority grants no source rights.

implementations: The census records root-license absence and conflicts rather than inferring clearance.
major_gaps: Zero external files are currently cleared.
capabilities.purpose: Prevent unreviewed code and assets entering a release.
required_tests: Fail build for every imported byte absent from cleared_files and attribution manifests.
security_notes: Owner authorization excludes third-party, forked, copied, generated, and vendored material.

implementations: The checked-in default-deny policy is enforced by source/archive/Pages checks and the full local publication-scan implementation over explicit source, history, Pages, release, redownload, and Actions-log inputs.
major_gaps: Two signed scanner gate executions, live repository settings, deployed Pages, public redownload, and completed Actions evidence remain externally unattested.
capabilities.purpose: Make publication default-deny across all surfaces.
required_tests: Scan current tree, history, archives, Pages, releases, source maps, and workflow output.
security_notes: Encryption, hashing, or ignore files do not make private data publishable.

implementations: The spine models 47 repositories and 59 protocols with structured gaps.
major_gaps: It omits direct load-bearing findings and leaves 35/59 required materials unresolved.
capabilities.purpose: Route situations to claimed protocols and expose drift.
required_tests: Compare spine nodes with the 307-repository census and report omissions without inheritance.
security_notes: Cross-check output is not source or authority.

implementations: Multiple canonicalizers and vectors exist.
major_gaps: Eternity code-point ordering can differ from RFC 8785 UTF-16 ordering.
capabilities.purpose: Produce stable bytes for hashes and signatures.
required_tests: Run RFC 8785 vectors including non-BMP keys across all implementations.
security_notes: A canonicalization mismatch breaks identity and signature verification.

implementations: Cards, facets, binders, and federation examples are deployed.
major_gaps: Binder version, mnemonic ordering, SVG sanitization, and import validation drift.
capabilities.purpose: Publish human-facing identity and capabilities.
required_tests: Validate schema, seed/ID binding, safe SVG, and peer-return identity.
security_notes: Cards are claims, not authenticated authority.

implementations: UUID, 16-, 32-, 64-hex and legacy 128-bit identities are live.
major_gaps: No safe universal migration or comparison rule exists.
capabilities.purpose: Recognize historical identities without silently equating them.
required_tests: Reject unsupported versions and prove no truncation or display-name aliasing.
security_notes: Display owner/slug equality must not imply cryptographic identity equality.

implementations: Lineage files exist in planted twins and distros.
major_gaps: Self-cycles and incompatible parent fields exist.
capabilities.purpose: Record parentage without changing identity equality.
required_tests: Reject cycles; preserve immutable identity; test missing and conflicting parents.
security_notes: Lineage is metadata and cannot authorize a parent.

implementations: Messaging code has logical identities and local state, but no selected target binding exists.
major_gaps: Final RAPPID/key fixture is unresolved.
capabilities.purpose: Bind an installed identity to the local OS owner.
required_tests: Prove wrong OS user, principal, RAPPID, or key fails closed.
security_notes: OS ownership, RAPPID, messaging principal, and transport key are distinct bindings.

implementations: P-256 event signing exists in protocol and browser/cloud implementations.
major_gaps: Key package, final fixtures, response signing, and replay behavior are unresolved or inconsistent.
capabilities.purpose: Authenticate twin requests and responses.
required_tests: Positive/negative vectors for signatures, key encoding, binding, rotation, and unknown keys.
security_notes: Private keys stay in explicit private state; public-key presence alone does not establish RAPPID binding.

implementations: The canonical 64-hex format is specified.
major_gaps: Live estate and planted twins still use legacy IDs.
capabilities.purpose: Give a twin a location-independent content-addressed name.
required_tests: Mint deterministic fixture; reject changed content, wrong width, and location-coupled variants.
security_notes: A content address authenticates bytes only under a correct canonicalization and mint policy.

implementations: Normative vectors exist; OpenRappter contains partial trust projection.
major_gaps: Some projection code is unreachable and consent capabilities omit required bindings.
capabilities.purpose: Project only facts authorized for the current principal and audience.
required_tests: Audience, memory ID, roster epoch, expiry, one-shot use, revocation, and no-leak vectors.
security_notes: A signed caller is not consent to disclose memory.

implementations: Local/Azure storage, agents, endpoints, installers, and tests exist.
major_gaps: Dynamic cloud code and broad CORS weaken the production boundary.
capabilities.purpose: Provide persistent conversational memory.
required_tests: Isolation, deletion, provenance, audience, restart, and injection tests.
security_notes: Memory content inherits the most restrictive source classification.

implementations: Concrete local and Azure storage adapters exist.
major_gaps: Identity/authorization and export privacy are not uniformly proved.
capabilities.purpose: Move the same memory abstraction between local and Azure stores.
required_tests: Cross-store equivalence, tenant isolation, least privilege, and no-private-export tests.
security_notes: Never treat a caller-supplied GUID as authorization.

implementations: Several repositories persist rich state, sometimes in public or weakly protected locations.
major_gaps: Public twin vaults and browser storage contradict privacy claims.
capabilities.purpose: Keep memory, journals, outbox, and identity state private.
required_tests: Permission, redaction, repository/history absence, and public-artifact negative scans.
security_notes: State is never an artifact input; secrets use Keychain.

implementations: Session IDs and user/shared memory patterns are implemented.
major_gaps: Historical default/shared GUID behavior can merge callers.
capabilities.purpose: Maintain local conversation continuity without cross-user leakage.
required_tests: Session isolation, expiry, restart policy, bounds, and identifier redaction.
security_notes: Session identifiers are private and are not authentication.

implementations: Durable echo-suppression state exists.
major_gaps: Full target owner-only sequence is not attested.
capabilities.purpose: Avoid processing the twin own outbound message as new input.
required_tests: Send/reobserve, restart, duplicate row, ambiguous delivery, and false-positive tests.
security_notes: Echo keys and message identifiers remain private.

implementations: The protocol discusses broader principals/groups; implementation is incomplete.
major_gaps: No selected safe group roster/epoch or SMS trust implementation exists.
capabilities.purpose: Handle groups and SMS downgrade.
required_tests: Prove all group, other-principal, and SMS events are rejected before model access.
security_notes: SMS changes transport trust; group membership changes memory audience.

implementations: OpenRappter has initial state/service code with WAL, cursor, lease, outbox, and recovery.
The target implements the narrowed owner-only adapter, pinned installer, supervised RPC, durable state, global-controller route, and mocked behavioral vectors.
major_gaps: Private owner enrollment and the final supported-host live route remain unattested.
capabilities.purpose: Read one owner conversation and send replies through Messages.
required_tests: Pinned binary, DB read-only, cursor, direct-message filter, send, restart, and concurrent writer tests.
security_notes: Messages database access is read-only; identifiers/content never leave private state.

implementations: Staged replies, leases, lowest pending recovery, and unknown-send handling exist.
The target implements explicit staged/flushed/submitted/unknown/not_sent transitions, restart recovery, and no-resend ambiguity tests.
major_gaps: The final live crash/sleep-wake attestation remains pending.
capabilities.purpose: Make reply delivery durable across crashes and unknown sends.
required_tests: Crash at each transition, ambiguous send, FIFO, lease expiry, duplicate, and journal corruption.
security_notes: Retry must not duplicate side effects or expose content in diagnostics.

implementations: Upstream has principal restrictions but broader scope.
The target enforces one exact owner self-chat and rejects other principals, groups, SMS, attachments, reactions, unknown chats, and unsupported event shapes before model access.
major_gaps: A real owner fixture is intentionally private and remains a live enrollment gate.
capabilities.purpose: Admit only one enrolled owner direct conversation.
required_tests: Owner positive; other principal, group, SMS, attachment, unsupported service, and changed identity negatives.
security_notes: Reject before content reaches memory, model, or agents.

implementations: Fourteen structural MUST vectors and green schema verification exist.
The target executes the narrowed owner-only authority, delivery, echo, privacy, and controller-routing vectors in stdlib tests.
major_gaps: Broader consent/group behavior remains excluded and live v1 attestation remains pending.
capabilities.purpose: Constrain consent, memory, tools, delivery, authority, and echoes.
required_tests: Execute every positive and negative behavioral vector against the target adapter.
security_notes: Passing verify.py is not messaging conformance.

implementations: Browser, Python, PeerJS, and cloud clients exist.
major_gaps: Protocol contradicts itself on keys; readers, replay, kinds, size, and license drift.
capabilities.purpose: Broadcast and discover signed public events.
required_tests: Signature/read verification, monotonicity, kinds, bounds, rate, dedupe, and key storage.
security_notes: Private keys in local files/browser storage and unverified reads are unsafe.

implementations: A live static estate and browser parser exist.
major_gaps: The spec admits live 32-hex location-coupled IDs violate the 64-hex invariant.
capabilities.purpose: Advertise all twins owned by an operator.
required_tests: Canonical identity, URL correctness, stale entry, rollback, signature, and offline discovery.
security_notes: Discovery metadata is not liveness, authorization, or backup proof.

implementations: A normative metropolis document exists with one estate instance.
major_gaps: No conformance validator or interoperable multi-estate implementation exists.
capabilities.purpose: Aggregate estates and neighborhoods into a broader discovery layer.
required_tests: Multiple estate fixtures, duplicate identities, conflicts, cycles, rollback, and unavailable peers.
security_notes: Aggregation magnifies stale identity and malicious-index risk.

implementations: A comprehensive MIT protocol document exists.
major_gaps: Replay algorithm is incomplete and broader plaintext/console modes conflict with CUBBY.
capabilities.purpose: Define peers, channels, twin-chat, pairing, sealing, and lifecycle.
required_tests: Run pinned envelope/wrapper vectors and target-specific rejection vectors.
security_notes: CUBBY selects signed loopback twin traffic only; no shell or plaintext say path.

implementations: Azure relay verifies signed posts and exposes room reads.
major_gaps: Event kind conflicts, sealed-body size bypass, no rate/total limits, and freshness gaps.
capabilities.purpose: Persist room events for late joiners.
required_tests: Signature, allowed kinds, total/body bounds, rate, pagination, freshness, and tenant isolation.
security_notes: Relay storage is public/cloud and must not receive CUBBY private data.

implementations: AES-256-GCM/PBKDF2 codec and negative vectors are executable.
major_gaps: Shared weak PIN deployments lack peer identity and replay protection.
capabilities.purpose: Encrypt a neighborhood body independently of transport.
required_tests: Cross-language round-trip, tamper, wrong key, unique IV, context binding, replay, and bounds.
security_notes: One shared secret lets any member impersonate another without signatures.

implementations: Several live front doors seal and post room events.
major_gaps: Six-digit PINs, fixed salts, missing read verification, and unsupported custom kinds.
capabilities.purpose: Operate a private brandable room through a front door.
required_tests: Offline-guess resistance, authenticated host, read verification, custom kinds, and no browser keys.
security_notes: Links embedding PINs and same-origin private keys defeat the privacy claim.

implementations: Frame envelopes, edge loop, raw/Issues transport, twins, and foundry exist.
major_gaps: Truncated hashes, unsigned pointers, rollback, unverified echoes, and schema mismatch exist.
capabilities.purpose: Coordinate intermittently connected autonomous nodes through frames.
required_tests: Full hash/signature, monotonic frame, trusted pointer, schema round-trip, and prompt-data isolation.
security_notes: Public telemetry can inject future model guidance.

implementations: Shell/operator bridges and canonical kite mark exist.
major_gaps: Legacy token possession permits console and sender strings are unverified.
capabilities.purpose: Connect browser/CDP/PeerJS operator surfaces to local chat.
required_tests: No console/eval, paired identity, replay, bounded transport, and disconnect recovery.
security_notes: Kite is tooling, not a substitute for paired signed control.

implementations: Index generation, drift views, deterministic crawl, and verification tools exist.
major_gaps: Coverage is incomplete and generated indexes can misclassify direct reality.
capabilities.purpose: Orient users and detect ecosystem drift.
required_tests: Coverage against census, stale head, collision, missing source, and no-execution crawl tests.
security_notes: Observability output must never become source authority.

implementations: The signed release manifest, canonical SHA256SUMS, Store/egg member manifests, installed inventory, and Store binding cover every local artifact-chain node.
major_gaps: Final public release and downloaded-byte digests remain intentionally unresolved.
capabilities.purpose: Bind every artifact-chain node to exact bytes.
required_tests: Digest mismatch at each stage, trusted metadata mutation, installed manifest, and post-upload compare.
security_notes: A digest proves consistency only after trusted publication.

implementations: The exact-output workflow builds twice from immutable Git objects and locked descriptors, compares bytes, verifies pinned trust, hatches, attests explicit assets, and uploads explicit names.
major_gaps: The workflow has not produced a public attestation or release.
capabilities.purpose: Test, publish, and deploy one immutable artifact.
required_tests: One build ID, no rebuild, checksum/signature, release download, Pages compare, and revocation.
security_notes: Signing/provenance must cover the exact tested bytes.

implementations: Destructive macOS/Windows tests and automatic main rollback scripts exist.
major_gaps: Manual ritual, mutable main assumptions, and no target CI integration.
capabilities.purpose: Validate fresh install and restore the prior release on failure.
required_tests: Fresh host, failed upgrade, exact prior digest restore, private state compatibility, and repeated rollback.
security_notes: Rollback must identify immutable releases and preserve private state safely.

implementations: Compile, tests, security, streaming, models, shell parsing, and fresh/upgrade jobs are defined.
Local deterministic implementation and stdlib tests exist; final public release attestation remains separate.
major_gaps: A workflow definition is not a target passing attestation.
capabilities.purpose: Reject unsupported or unsafe hosts before installation.
required_tests: Run exact target host checks and publish sanitized counts/version receipt only.
security_notes: Preflight must not print credentials or mutate before all blocking checks pass.

implementations: Per-file source/provenance manifests, adapted OpenRappter notices, SPDX file licensing, and lock equality are locally enforced.
major_gaps: Final public attestation remains outside the source provenance gate.
capabilities.purpose: Allow external bytes only after file-level review.
required_tests: Manifest/provenance equality, blob mismatch, attribution, excluded source, and generated-file lineage.
security_notes: Repository-level license never clears an unreviewed file.

implementations: The publication-scan command inventories tracked, untracked, and ignored candidates and scans reachable Git refs, blobs, commits, and annotated tags with fixed plumbing.

It recursively scans ZIP, egg, wheel, tar, and gzip members; exact generated Pages and release assets; public redownloads; and explicitly supplied Actions log ZIPs without checkout or target execution.
Canonical receipts are redacted to rule/artifact/member/path/digest metadata and can be detached-signed and verified against RELEASE_TRUST.json.
major_gaps: Candidate and final execution still require the release/Pages wiring task to supply real public redownload, deployed Pages, and complete Actions log exports.
No local result claims those external publication surfaces were scanned until both signed receipts exist.
capabilities.purpose: Scan every public surface and recursively unpacked artifact.
required_tests: Current/deleted-history secrets, UTF-16/base64/URL, identifiers, paths, high entropy, nested/corrupt/bomb archives, source maps, Actions logs, deterministic receipt/signature/trust/tamper, and zero-finding candidate surface.
security_notes: Raw values never enter findings or public receipts; unsupported/corrupt archives, special files, traversal, and limits fail closed.
Release mode requires a real commit and complete history; not_available_development is accepted only for development.

implementations: File discovery, import, contract checks, and stack loaders exist.
major_gaps: Existing loaders may execute untrusted code while validating it.
capabilities.purpose: Discover internal single-file agents at runtime.
required_tests: Discover exact manifest only; reject symlinks, extras, duplicate tools, and import side effects.
security_notes: Discovery is code execution and must consume only build-approved files.

implementations: The minimal Python ABI is directly implemented and tested in several repositories.
major_gaps: Context/data-slush claims in older docs exceed the shipped ABI.
capabilities.purpose: Define the executable unit shared by brainstems.
required_tests: Import, instantiate, convert, invoke, reject malformed metadata, and run standalone.
security_notes: Agent metadata and parameter schemas are untrusted input until validated.

implementations: The project runtime under src/rapp_stack_cubby/runtime is newly authored and exercised by local runtime/server/registry/provider tests.
Microsoft aibast material is behavioral and license reference only; no Microsoft or owner-copy runtime source is adapted into the project runtime.
major_gaps: Final public exact-commit and artifact attestations remain release gates; they do not change implementation origin.
capabilities.purpose: Provide the original clean-room local brainstem runtime selected by this product.
required_tests: Runtime, registry, provider, server, tool-loop, provenance-origin, and excluded-external-source checks.
security_notes: External runtime projects are reference-only. Copying or adapting their source would violate the selected provenance boundary.

implementations: Several runtimes save/import uploaded Python and auto-install missing packages.
major_gaps: No containment or publisher trust boundary makes this safe.
capabilities.purpose: Import or upload new agent code while running.
required_tests: Assert import/upload routes do not exist and request handling cannot invoke package managers.
security_notes: Broad bind plus CORS plus import is host-user RCE.

implementations: Stdlib /run, /eval, /chat and browser/Pyodide variants exist.
major_gaps: Published variants expose eval/run and permissive CORS.
capabilities.purpose: Run agents without a browser or Flask dependency.
required_tests: Confirm prohibited eval/run routes are absent in the selected surface.
security_notes: Headless does not imply safe; arbitrary eval is prohibited.

implementations: Substantial local runtime and cross-platform preflight definitions exist.
major_gaps: Route breadth, remote import, auto-pip, dependency lower bounds, and absent license conflict with target.
capabilities.purpose: Provide current behavioral brainstem semantics and preflight.
required_tests: Behavioral parity vectors for approved ABI/tool loop/chat only.
security_notes: Never vendor from the pointer repository.

implementations: All major local runtimes implement a chat endpoint.
major_gaps: Request and response fields differ across frozen and current wires.
capabilities.purpose: Expose the sole v1 capability wire.
required_tests: Exact request/response schema, bounds, sessions, error shape, and prohibited-route tests.
security_notes: Only loopback local owner access is unsigned; privileged operations still require signatures.

implementations: The sole top-level RappStackCubbyController owns local child chat while child agents stay internal.
major_gaps: Release attestation remains pending.
capabilities.purpose: Stream only at the top-level controller boundary.
required_tests: Show only controller emits stream events and every internal agent remains discoverable/executable.
security_notes: Flattening child agents changes policy and execution semantics.

implementations: Multi-round tool loops are implemented.
major_gaps: Existing loops expose every loaded tool and have differing round/error behavior.
capabilities.purpose: Translate model tool calls into bounded agent invocations.
required_tests: Bound rounds, schema validation, tool errors, cancellation, and prohibited side effects.
security_notes: Tool output is untrusted model context and must not trigger dynamic code or privilege escalation.

implementations: Store checks are relatively strong; hatchers contain direct traversal and limit failures.
Local deterministic implementation and stdlib tests exist; final public release attestation remains separate.
major_gaps: No selected hardened extractor exists.
capabilities.purpose: Prevent an artifact from escaping or exhausting its install root.
required_tests: Absolute/relative traversal, separator variants, symlink/hardlink, devices, bombs, nesting, and cleanup.
security_notes: Reject links, special files, encrypted/unsupported members, nesting, count, and expansion excess.

implementations: Installer has meaningful protections; other copies bind broadly with wildcard CORS.
major_gaps: Upstream behavior is not uniformly hardened or selected.
capabilities.purpose: Prevent websites or LAN peers from driving the local runtime.
required_tests: LAN/wildcard bind, DNS rebinding, hostile Origin, preflight, Host, IPv4/IPv6, and request-size tests.
security_notes: Bind only 127.0.0.1/::1; disabled CORS or one exact local origin.

implementations: Direct reports show import, auto-pip, eval, console, and LLM-triggered hatch paths.
major_gaps: Those paths are common in reference implementations.
capabilities.purpose: Keep request/message input from becoming executable code.
required_tests: Route inventory, message/tool injection, import, auto-pip, subprocess, eval, mutable URL, and shell negatives.
security_notes: No signature grants arbitrary code-execution authority.

implementations: Direct evidence demonstrates the shared-origin blast radius.
The local dependency-free Pages site, deterministic APIs, CSP, and fail-closed checker enforce a static credential-free handoff.
major_gaps: Live public deployment and post-publication scan remain release-operator evidence.
capabilities.purpose: Keep identity, keys, messages, memory, and credentials off public web origins.
required_tests: Static output checks reject storage, service workers, loopback/private requests, token fields, maps, external executable resources, and weak CSP.
security_notes: CUBBY Pages runtime is excluded; static output must be credential-free.

implementations: Normative envelope/trust material exists.
major_gaps: P-256 package, key fixtures, target implementation, and attestation are unresolved.
protocol_or_contract: Pinned neighborhood wrapper plus CUBBY pairing/replay profile
capabilities.purpose: Authenticate all inter-twin requests and responses.
required_tests: Full positive/negative cryptographic, binding, response, replay, crash, and corruption suite.
security_notes: Fail closed on canonicalization, signature, binding, replay, or journal uncertainty.

implementations: Static catalog, validator, receiver, staging, human approval, and promotion are real.
major_gaps: Federated approved URLs can remain branch-mutable.
capabilities.purpose: Discover and approve application bundles.
required_tests: Traversal/bomb, AST, digest, immutable promotion, federation rollback, and approval tests.
security_notes: Approval must bind immutable bytes and publisher policy.

implementations: Three distinct repositories implement these catalog roles.
major_gaps: The ecosystem sometimes describes them as one store and licensing differs.
capabilities.purpose: Separate agent source, application bundles, and output senses.
required_tests: Reject cross-store schema substitution and require independent provenance.
security_notes: A listing in one store grants no trust in another.

implementations: IndexedDB/localStorage are used by real Pages applications.
major_gaps: Project paths do not isolate storage.
capabilities.purpose: Persist browser application state.
required_tests: Prove the public UI writes no private browser state.
security_notes: Any same-origin project can reach keys, tokens, identity, or location stored by another.

implementations: Pinned commits are recorded for behavior and candidate source.
major_gaps: Selected Microsoft file/blob manifest is unresolved.
capabilities.purpose: Identify source by immutable commit and blob.
required_tests: Match every imported destination to an approved upstream blob SHA.
security_notes: A branch URL or repository name is not source identity.

implementations: Workflows process issues, validate packages, build Pages, and release artifacts.
major_gaps: Workflow coverage and pin quality vary by repository.
capabilities.purpose: Use repository automation as deterministic compute.
required_tests: Pin actions; build once; compare deployed and released bytes.
security_notes: Writable workflows and mutable actions are supply-chain authority.

implementations: Rappterbook and frame systems use authenticated GitHub surfaces for writes.
major_gaps: Identity binding, replay ledgers, and schema versions are inconsistent.
capabilities.purpose: Carry public mutations and social events without a custom server.
required_tests: Bind actor to platform identity; reject replay and malformed events.
security_notes: Body-supplied identities and prompt-reflected telemetry are untrusted.

implementations: The census records 108 repositories with Pages enabled.
major_gaps: A static front door is often mistaken for a running twin.
capabilities.purpose: Publish static documentation, catalogs, front doors, and demos.
required_tests: Scan staged/deployed bytes; assert no loopback calls, tokens, storage, or service worker.
security_notes: Pages is one shared origin and must never carry runtime credentials or private state.

implementations: SQLite/local storage patterns exist; this project narrows permissions and placement.
major_gaps: No target runtime implementation or attestation exists yet.
capabilities.purpose: Hold installed twin state outside publication surfaces.
required_tests: Verify permissions, restrictive creation umask, atomic writes, and checkout exclusion.
security_notes: Private roots require 0700; file state requires 0600 and atomic replacement.

implementations: Installer preflight and postflight contain macOS paths.
major_gaps: The exact host attestation and dependency pins are unresolved.
capabilities.purpose: Provide the one v1 attested execution host.
required_tests: Attest OS/build, architecture, Python, Git, gh, Messages, Copilot access, and binaries.
security_notes: Host tools and Messages access are privileged local boundaries.

implementations: Static registries, indexes, and conformance scoring are implemented.
major_gaps: Many published URLs still follow mutable branches.
capabilities.purpose: Treat versioned repository files as zero-server APIs.
required_tests: Resolve immutable URL; verify schema, digest, publisher, and rollback state.
security_notes: Hash integrity does not authenticate an attacker-controlled catalog.

implementations: The controller starts a dedicated child process group, binds health to a random instance ID, and performs bounded exact-group stop.
major_gaps: Signed privileged control and supported-host release attestation remain pending.
capabilities.purpose: Supervise the exact installed child process.
required_tests: Port collision, stale PID, wrong process, crash, restart, and graceful/forced stop tests.
security_notes: Stop must bind PID/process receipt, manifest, workspace, and listener—not port alone.

implementations: Freeze/lay/hatch utilities exist.
Local deterministic implementation and stdlib tests exist; final public release attestation remains separate.
major_gaps: Identity, state privacy, signature, and extraction policies differ.
capabilities.purpose: Round-trip a child for recovery or transfer.
required_tests: Quiesce, deterministic package, private-state exclusion, restore, and installed-manifest equality.
security_notes: Freezing private state does not make it public.

implementations: The controller exact-commit hatcher creates identity-keyed private source, workspace, data, agents, soul, session, and process state.
major_gaps: Production/public rehatch remains blocked until packaging supplies the release source manifest and final release SHA.
capabilities.purpose: Turn one local controller into isolated project twins.
required_tests: Two-child isolation, dependency conflict, identity separation, fan-out, and teardown tests.
security_notes: Each child needs separate dependencies, identity, keys, state, and process.

implementations: Existing hatchers create workspaces and runnable twins.
The selected local path verifies an egg installation, mints a private instance RAPPID, adopts exact installed source/venv bytes, and starts only through the controller.
major_gaps: Live/public artifact attestation remains separate from local adoption.
capabilities.purpose: Install one verified artifact into one dedicated environment.
required_tests: Filesystem containment, exact dependencies, no shared mutation, and installed manifest attestation.
security_notes: A venv is dependency isolation, not an OS sandbox.

implementations: Identity and bond minting code exists in incompatible forms.
major_gaps: Final target RAPPID/key fixture is unresolved.
capabilities.purpose: Create a stable identity and local transport binding.
required_tests: Deterministic RAPPID, distinct key binding, collision, lineage, and fixture publication review.
security_notes: Identity minting must be deterministic and cannot leak private key material.

implementations: Many static planted repositories are live.
major_gaps: Frozen mirrors, mutable installers, and identity drift are common.
capabilities.purpose: Publish an invitation/card for a twin or place.
required_tests: Display offline state, immutable installer link, identity match, and no private browser state.
security_notes: A front door is not runtime liveness or authority.

implementations: Installer/adoption, supervision, and chat UI exist.
major_gaps: Mutable shell installers, no tests, and unsigned/no-check distribution paths.
capabilities.purpose: Hide terminal/browser setup behind a desktop app.
required_tests: Signed/notarized immutable package, update verification, renderer isolation, and no mutable scripts.
security_notes: Desktop packaging expands update, signing, and renderer trust boundaries.

implementations: Local chat surfaces exist across major runtimes.
major_gaps: No target UI or attestation yet.
capabilities.purpose: Give the local owner a direct conversational surface.
required_tests: Fresh install to first response, errors, accessibility, no extra routes, and private-state scan.
security_notes: UI must be exact-origin or same-process and hold no long-lived secrets in browser storage.

implementations: Many live Pages front doors and planted identities exist.
major_gaps: They often embed mutable installers, weak PINs, or stale mirrors.
capabilities.purpose: Invite, document, or download without operating the runtime.
required_tests: Offline/liveness labeling, immutable download, no secrets/storage, CSP, and link integrity.
security_notes: Never present a static page as proof a twin is running or secure.

implementations: Pyodide ZIP execution and a concrete rapplication cartridge exist.
major_gaps: No safe extraction, digest/signature/schema checks, tests, or durable/eject features.
capabilities.purpose: Drop/fetch a cartridge and run it in a browser console.
required_tests: SRI/fixed assets, safe ZIP, signature, schema, limits, sandbox, eject, and persistence.
security_notes: CDN/Pyodide and arbitrary Python are executable supply-chain boundaries.

implementations: Boot/install, previews, twin registry, and controls are implemented.
major_gaps: No tests; mutable installers; arbitrary local HTML executes scripts without CSP.
capabilities.purpose: Boot, inspect, and control twins from an editor.
required_tests: Immutable install, process binding, webview CSP/sanitization, tests, and workspace trust.
security_notes: Editor extension has host-user privileges and must not render untrusted active content.

determinism.array_order: capabilities sorted by id; source repositories sorted case-insensitively by name

direct_evidence_methodology.authority: Repository-local evidence at the audited default-branch head outranks indexes, mirrors, titles, and canonical claims.
direct_evidence_methodology.head_semantics: Evidence locators remain pinned to evidence_head_sha. current_head_sha is a per-repository API observation at head_observed_at inside the declared non-atomic window; post-window movement is separate.
direct_evidence_methodology.inspection: Read-only exact-head tree, README, specification/manifest, relevant implementation, root-license, and Pages inspection completed for all twelve required drift repositories; records are local in the audit manifest and shards.
direct_evidence_methodology.local_product_semantics: This repository is a separate local product node absent from the antecedent public account snapshot. Local tested claims cite repository paths, not a fabricated antecedent repo.
direct_evidence_methodology.observation_window: Bounded and non-atomic; inventory pages and repository heads have distinct recorded request/response times.
direct_evidence_methodology.spine_policy: The canonical spine was consulted only after direct evidence and only as a non-authoritative cross-check.

local_product.claim: Selected local implementations are proved by this repository and are not attributed to a public antecedent repository.

status_definitions.contradictory: Direct implementations or normative claims conflict materially.
status_definitions.deprecated: Explicitly retired, frozen, archived, or superseded.
status_definitions.implemented: Substantial executable implementation exists; this does not imply CUBBY selection or conformance.
status_definitions.partial: Some executable behavior exists, but required portions or proof are missing.
status_definitions.spec_only: Normative or descriptive material exists without an implementation proving it.
status_definitions.unsafe_legacy: Executable behavior exists but has a direct high-impact trust or security failure.
